description_long: This induction cooker features a sleek, rectangular black glass-ceramic surface with four distinct circular heating zones outlined in white. The cooktop is flush and smooth, with no raised elements, and the control panel is centrally located along one long edge, featuring red digital touch controls and illuminated icons. The control panel includes a digital display, several touch-sensitive buttons for power and temperature adjustment, and clear burner indicators. The surface is highly reflective, giving it a glossy, modern appearance, and the edges are slightly beveled for a seamless integration into countertops. No physical knobs or switches are present, emphasizing a minimalist, contemporary design. There are no visible brand markings or power cords, and the cooktop appears to be designed for built-in installation in urban kitchens or communal cooking spaces.
description: A modern, black glass-ceramic induction cooktop with four outlined burners and a central red digital touch control panel. The surface is glossy, flat, and minimalist, with no visible brand or power cord.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full rectangular cooktop, all four burners, and the central red digital control panel clearly.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the depth of the cooktop and the arrangement of the burners, with the control panel visible along the near edge.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the cooktop surface and burner layout, with the control panel less visible and no additional features on the far edge.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view, mirroring view 1, with the control panel now on the far edge and the burner arrangement visible from a different perspective.
attribute_control_panel_layout: The control panel is centrally located along one long edge of the cooktop, featuring a horizontal row of red illuminated touch-sensitive buttons and a digital display. The panel includes icons for each burner, power, and temperature adjustment, all arranged in a linear, easy-to-access format.
